untranslatable_reason: This signal requires option-specific calculations and data (in-the-money status, time value, interest on settlement credit, days to expiration, risk-free rate) that are not available in the standard equity/futures indicator set. The rule requires comparing interest earned on a credited balance against remaining option time value—calculations that depend on derivatives pricing models and account-specific data, not price action indicators.
translation_notes: NATENBERG_DRY_113_0 is an options exercise decision rule from volatility/derivatives theory. It requires: (1) option moneyness detection, (2) time value extraction from option prices, (3) interest accrual on settlement credits, (4) days-to-expiration tracking, and (5) risk-free rate input. None of these are expressible using the allowed indicators (price, moving averages, RSI, ATR, etc.). This rule belongs in an options-specific backtest engine with option chain data and Greeks calculations, not a standard price-action backtester.
